game.name: stag_hunt
game.description: A cooperative BitWorld hunting game where players surround prey together: rabbits go down alone, but stags, moose, and elephants require coordinated multi-player encirclement.

tokens.description: One connection token per player slot, indexed by slot. The certified shape is a fixed 4-hunter roster (the largest coalition, an elephant, needs 4), so multi-player coordination across the full prey range is exercisable.

maxTicks.description: Maximum simulation ticks per round (24 ticks/sec), where 0 means no tick limit.

maxGames.description: Maximum fresh games before shutdown, where 0 means no game limit.

names.description: One player name or address per player slot.

scores.description: Cumulative hunting score per player slot across all rounds.

catches.description: Per-player array of [rabbit, boar, stag, moose, elephant] capture counts.

co_captures.description: NxN matrix of shared kills between player slots.

content.value:
# Stag Hunt Rules

Stag Hunt is a cooperative grid-hunting game. Players (hunters) move around a tile world and capture prey by surrounding them. The catch is the coordination: small prey can be taken alone, but larger prey require a coalition of hunters encircling them at the same time.

## Prey and coalitions

| Prey | Hunters needed |
| --- | --- |
| Rabbit | 1 (solo) |
| Boar | 2 |
| Stag | 2 |
| Moose | 3 |
| Elephant | 4 |

Larger prey are worth more, so there is a real stag-hunt tension: a hunter can take guaranteed small rabbits alone, or gamble on coordinating with others for a much bigger reward. Moose and elephants also actively fight back (gut/trample), draining the energy of poorly-coordinated hunters.

## Scoring

Each capture awards score (and energy) to every participating hunter. A player's result is their cumulative score across all rounds. Shared kills are recorded per player pair, so cooperative play is visible in the results.

## Energy

Hunters spend energy to act and gain it from catches. Running out of energy is punishing, which is why steady rabbit income competes against the high-variance payoff of big coordinated hunts.

player.name: Big Game Hunter
player.description: Counts nearby allies, derives which prey kinds are catchable at that coalition size, and picks the highest-reward catchable prey.

player.name: Nearest Hunter
player.description: Greedy bot that always chases the closest reachable prey using A* pathfinding.

player.name: Rabbiteer
player.description: Specialist bot that prioritizes rabbits for guaranteed solo kills and consistent energy income.

player.name: Sidekick
player.description: Cooperative bot that follows nearby allies and assists with encirclement of larger prey.

player.name: Stag Hunter
player.description: Mid-tier specialist that targets stags and boars, balancing reward size against required coordination.

player.name: Moose Hunter
player.description: Specialist bot that targets moose, using rank-based side assignment to coordinate three hunters approaching from different cardinal directions.

player.name: Elephant Hunter
player.description: Specialist bot that targets elephants exclusively, using rank-based side assignment to coordinate four hunters into a simultaneous encirclement.

player.name: Modeler
player.description: Adaptive bot that scores all prey types by reward, distance, and learned ally cooperation probability, dynamically choosing the highest-expected-value target.

reporter.name: Stag Hunt Commentator
reporter.description: Sports-commentator / newspaper write-up of an episode. Computes reward-agnostic facts (actual scores, prey breakdown, who teamed with whom, lead changes) and narrates them with Claude via AWS Bedrock, falling back to a deterministic templated recap when Bedrock is unavailable.

grader.name: Stag Hunt Collaboration Grader
grader.description: Reward-agnostic scalar score (0-1) for how interesting an episode was w.r.t. multi-agent collaboration: cooperation depth and breadth from the co-capture structure, competitive balance from actual scores, and prey diversity. Does not hardcode prey point values or coalition sizes, so it survives server-side rebalancing.

grader.name: BitWorld Score Grader
grader.description: Generic BitWorld grader that scores episode interest from score spread and game-specific activity signals.

variants.name: Smoke
variants.description: Short deterministic Stag Hunt configuration used for manifest checks.
